name: node-promise-retry
title: node-promise-retry
summary: Retries a function that returns a promise
description:
Retries a function that returns a promise
Leverage the power of the retry module to the promises world.
.
Calls fn until the returned promise ends up fulfilled or rejected with an
error different than a retry error.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-promise-retry because Retries a function that returns a promise.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-promise-retry as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-promise-retry with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-promise-retry --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
